component: optimization_level=3 two-qubit resynthesis (Consolidate Blocks / unitary synthesis)
severity: minor — small numerical error, NOT a wrong-answer bug; contract question
status: discovered, secondary finding, not yet reported
found_by: cforge-fuzz Qiskit campaign, N1 amplitude oracle, seed 777
expected: cp(theta) = diag(1,1,1,e^{i*theta}); exact decomposition uses 2 CX
observed: at optimization_level=3 with approximation_degree=1.0 (documented 'no approximation'), cp(theta) for |theta| below ~1e-3 is synthesized as two single-qubit rz gates with ZERO CX, dropping the controlled interaction. N1 error ~ theta/4 (2.4e-5 here). optimization_level<=1 preserves both CX exactly.
reproducer_python:
import numpy as np
from qiskit import QuantumCircuit, transpile
from qiskit.quantum_info import Operator
qc = QuantumCircuit(2); qc.cp(9.623e-05, 0, 1)
t = transpile(qc, basis_gates=['rz','sx','x','cx'], optimization_level=3, approximation_degree=1.0)
print([i.operation.name for i in t.data])   # ['rz','rz'] — no cx
notes: This is a WEAK finding versus qiskit-commutative-cancellation-001: the error is O(theta) and tiny, and one can argue a near-identity two-qubit unitary is legitimately synthesized as a product of 1-qubit gates.
The sharp part: it happens even at approximation_degree=1.0, which the docs call 'no approximation'. So it is a contract/documentation question, not a dramatic soundness bug.
Distinct root cause from entry 001 (that one is CommutativeCancellation on sxdg; this is 2q unitary resynthesis thresholding).
Report only if the Qiskit maintainers, on entry 001, express interest in edge cases — bundling a weak finding with the strong one risks diluting the strong one.
